input.new_string:
- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/{train,validation,test}.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (2,143 messages across 167/4414 train (3.8%), 65/519 validation (12.5%), 20/261 test (7.7%) conversations) — likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`, so this is invisible to existing validation. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: add a role-enum check to `data_validator.py` and filter/repair affected rows before any further Cat A SFT retrain — this contaminates 12.5% of the validation split the 0.674 held-out baseline was measured against.

- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/{train,validation,test}.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (2,143 messages across 167/4414 train (3.8%), 65/519 validation (12.5%), 20/261 test (7.7%) conversations) — likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`, so this is invisible to existing validation. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. - R11: Gemma-4 12B (`gemma4_unified`) + vLLM

lines: - R8: Gemma4 + TurboQuant → **works with perf caveat**. Two hooks in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py`: (1) `_install_gemma4_mixed_backend_hook` bypasses the TRITON_ATTN global force at `vllm/model_executor/models/config.py:100`; (2) `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` auto-injects `enforce_eager=True` for Gemma-4 + `turboquant_*`, skipping vLLM v1's broken mixed-KV profiler (`gpu_model_runner.py:6598`). CUDA graphs off → expect ~15-25% decode throughput penalty vs graph-mode. Numerical correctness unaffected. Restore graph-mode once upstream lands a per-layer-aware profiler.

- R9: Gemma-4 (26B-A4B, 31B) + Unsloth `fast_inference=True` → **architecture allowlist rejection**. Unsloth 2026.5.2's `VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at `unsloth/models/vision.py:242` is hardcoded to `{qwen2_5_vl, gemma3, mistral3, qwen3_vl, qwen3_vl_moe}`. Gemma-4 ships as a SigLIP+Gemma4 multimodal stack, so `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(..., fast_inference=True)` raises `RuntimeError: Found architectures: siglip, gemma4!` (`unsloth/models/vision.py:610`). **Auto-resolved.** `training/grpo.py::_detect_model_family` reads the SFT checkpoint's `adapter_config.json → base_model_name_or_path` and resolves `AutoConfig.model_type`. When the family is in `UNSLOTH_VLLM_INCOMPATIBLE_FAMILIES = {"gemma4"}`, `use_vllm` is forced to `False` with a `vllm_rollout_disabled_unsloth_incompat` warning; training proceeds with HF `model.generate()` rollouts (~31 s/step on H100 80GB → ~8.6 h per 1000-step GRPO run). Other Cat A models (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Mistral-Small-3.1-24B, Qwen3.6 variants) are unaffected — they continue to get Unsloth colocate vLLM rollouts. To re-enable vLLM rollouts on Gemma-4: (a) wait for Unsloth to add `gemma4` to the allowlist (file an issue at github.com/unslothai/unsloth), (b) switch to external vLLM server mode (`vllm_mode="server"` + a separate `trl vllm-serve` process — tight on 80GB but viable with FP8 weights), or (c) monkey-patch `unsloth.models.vision.VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at import time (quick to try, may fail deeper in vLLM-engine init).

- R10: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B + vLLM → **no tagged release supports it** (verified 2026-06-11: vLLM 0.22.1 model registry has no `diffusion_gemma` entry; transformers added the model defs in 5.11.0; the HF checkpoints ship no remote code). Serving works ONLY via vLLM's `dgemma` branch (PR #45163, open) — prebuilt images `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma-cu129` (CUDA 12.9) / `:gemma` (CUDA 13.0, driver ≥ 580). Required flags per the RedHatAI model card: `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--max-num-seqs 4`, `--hf-overrides '{"diffusion_sampler": "entropy_bound", "diffusion_entropy_bound": 0.1}'`. Wired into `deployments/models/` (`VLLM_IMAGE` + `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER` + `VLLM_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE` knobs; preset in `.env.example`). `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) cannot serve it — fails at ModelConfig validation ("model type `diffusion_gemma` ... not recognize this architecture"). Keep `kv-cache-dtype` blank (fp8 KV on the diffusion decode path unvalidated). Re-pin to a tagged image once PR #45163 merges into a release.

+- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/{train,validation,test}.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (2,143 messages across 167/4414 train (3.8%), 65/519 validation (12.5%), 20/261 test (7.7%) conversations) — likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`, so this is invisible to existing validation. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: add a role-enum check to `data_validator.py` and filter/repair affected rows before any further Cat A SFT retrain — this contaminates 12.5% of the validation split the 0.674 held-out baseline was measured against.

- R11: Gemma-4 12B (`gemma4_unified`) + vLLM → **needs vLLM ≥ v0.23.0** (verified 2026-07-06 against the model registry: `Gemma4Unified*` absent through v0.22.1, present in v0.23.0/v0.24.0). The pinned serving image (`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0`) and `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) both fail at ModelConfig validation ("model type `gemma4_unified` ... does not recognize this architecture"). On L4 24GB the BF16 checkpoint can never fit regardless (11.96B params ≈ 23.9 GB weights > 22.03 GiB usable) — use `RedHatAI/gemma-4-12B-it-FP8-Dynamic` (~12.75 GB load, compressed-tensors FP8, tested by RedHat against vLLM nightly) with `VLLM_IMAGE=vllm/vllm-openai:v0.24.0`. Verified serving on L4 (2026-07-06): fp8 KV, `--max-num-batched-tokens 4096`, 50,087-token KV cache at 8K max_model_len; tool calling via parser `gemma4` works; also verified with LMCache + `--enforce-eager`. Preset in `.env.example`.

**How to apply:** don't recommend a straight SFT retrain on this checkpoint without first doing
the forensics above — the loss-mask fix is unverified and a confounded retrain would waste GPU
time without attributable signal.

**Forensics result (2026-07-14) — found a real, independent data-corruption bug (CLAUDE.md R12).**
`data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/*.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like
`assistant to=tool` and empty content — 2,143 messages, contaminating 3.8% of train / **12.5% of
validation** / 7.7% of test conversations. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message
(exactly where the model must decide to narrate vs. call the next tool). `data_validator.py`
never validates the role enum past `messages[0]`, so this passed cleaning untouched. Gemma-4's
chat template doesn't reject unknown roles — it renders them literally — so under
`loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trained with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty
turns. Full detail in `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1.
**How to apply:** fix this (add a role-enum validator check + filter/repair affected rows) before
any Cat A SFT retrain or before trusting the 0.674 held-out baseline — it's measured against a

toolUseResult.newString:
**How to apply:** don't recommend a straight SFT retrain on this checkpoint without first doing
the forensics above — the loss-mask fix is unverified and a confounded retrain would waste GPU
time without attributable signal.

**Forensics result (2026-07-14) — found a real, independent data-corruption bug (CLAUDE.md R12).**
`data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/*.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like
`assistant to=tool` and empty content — 2,143 messages, contaminating 3.8% of train / **12.5% of
validation** / 7.7% of test conversations. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message
(exactly where the model must decide to narrate vs. call the next tool). `data_validator.py`
never validates the role enum past `messages[0]`, so this passed cleaning untouched. Gemma-4's
chat template doesn't reject unknown roles — it renders them literally — so under
`loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trained with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty
turns. Full detail in `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1.
**How to apply:** fix this (add a role-enum validator check + filter/repair affected rows) before
any Cat A SFT retrain or before trusting the 0.674 held-out baseline — it's measured against a
validation split that's 12.5% contaminated. This is now the top-priority action, ahead of the
loss-mask A/B and upsampling experiments. Checks #2 (finish_reason on the actual audit rows) and
the headroom/pass@k re-probes still need the real GPU host — this session had no CUDA and no
merged weights, only the LoRA adapter.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: project-grpo-cat-a-stall
description: Phase 2 Cat A GRPO (Gemma-4-26B-A4B) fine-tuning is stalled at an unresolved maintainer decision fork as of 2026-07-14
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 82ed40c5-aaa3-46c6-8fc8-378f94366a67
---

Single-turn online GRPO on Gemma-4-26B-A4B Cat A does not work, and the trajectory-reward
pivot tried as a fallback also came back MARGINAL on every cheap knob. See
`docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md` and `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` for the
full record.

**Why:** Reward lattice is too coarse — most 8-sample GRPO groups collapse to a single reward
rung, so the advantage (and gradient) is exactly zero on ~70%+ of groups. A headroom probe
(2026-07-09), a trajectory-variance probe re-run at n=150 (2026-07-10), and a final T=0.8→1.0
knob (most recent commit, `d9d8b44`/`4c7f89d`) all landed MARGINAL, never clearing GO. The
SFT-only ceiling check was also checked as an alternative — after fixing a scoring-granularity
bug (per-turn-fair composite + GT sanitizer for fabricated tool args), the honest baseline is
0.674 composite, still under the 0.75 target.

**How to apply:** The trajectory track's cheap knobs are now exhausted per the recorded
2026-07-10 routing decision. Three paths remain, and choosing between them is an explicitly
**deferred maintainer decision**, not yet made as of the last commit:
1. Fall back to single-turn RFT (rejection-sampling fine-tuning, Path #1 in the viability memo).
2. Ship SFT-only for Phase 2 Cat A (accept the 0.674 ceiling).
3. Invest in full trainer wiring for the trajectory reward despite it not clearing GO.

When asked about Phase 2 / GRPO status, don't say "complete" — say this fork is open and ask
which path the user wants before doing further GRPO work. Verify against `git log` for newer
commits before relying on this, since this is a fast-moving track.

**Update (2026-07-14) — revised action plan after a Fable review of SFT-improvement options.**
The genuine (non-artifact) weakness is "announce-but-don't-call": on tool-expected held-out rows
the model narrates intent/state correctly but under-emits `<tool_call>`. First hypothesis was
that `checkpoint-1000` trained with `loss_mask=all_tokens` (gradient diluted across boilerplate
system prompt) instead of the already-implemented-but-never-used `response_only` recipe. Fable's
review found this plausible but unproven, and surfaced a real confound: ckpt-1000 trained
**before** the GT sanitizer (which drops 32 fabricated-arg tool calls) existed, so any retrain
changes both loss-recipe and data at once unless controlled. Revised, higher-priority plan before
spending GPU on a retrain:
1. Cheap forensics first: inspect rendered training rows for tool-expected turns (single-turn
   slicing may have literally created announce-only gold rows), check `finish_reason`/truncation
   on the 15 announce-but-don't-call rows, verify Gemma chat-template system-prompt folding.
2. Run the RFT headroom re-probe (already flagged as overdue in
   `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` §6) **now**, in parallel — it's nearly free and
   gates whether RFT is even viable regardless of SFT changes. Add a pass@k (T≈1.0) probe on the
   52 tool-expected held-out rows specifically — the real RFT-feasibility number, not yet measured.
3. If a masking/data experiment is still warranted, combine `response_only` + tool-turn upsampling
   into one factorial run on sanitized data rather than two sequential single-variable retrains.
4. Known measurement gaps to fix before trusting any delta: n=52 tool-expected rows → ~±13pp CI,
   too small to trust deltas under ~15pp; the 0.674 composite is on a different metric than the
   0.75 target it's being compared to (target needs re-deriving for the per-turn-fair scorer).

**How to apply:** don't recommend a straight SFT retrain on this checkpoint without first doing
the forensics above — the loss-mask fix is unverified and a confounded retrain would waste GPU
time without attributable signal.

the forensics above — the loss-mask fix is unverified and a confounded retrain would waste GPU
time without attributable signal.
+**Forensics result (2026-07-14) — found a real, independent data-corruption bug (CLAUDE.md R12).**
+`data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/*.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like
+`assistant to=tool` and empty content — 2,143 messages, contaminating 3.8% of train / **12.5% of
+validation** / 7.7% of test conversations. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message
+(exactly where the model must decide to narrate vs. call the next tool). `data_validator.py`

content.text:
**Bottom line:** the forensics surfaced a real, previously-unknown bug that outranks everything else in the plan — a data-corruption issue (malformed `assistant to=X` roles with empty content, contaminating 3.8% of train / **12.5% of validation** / 7.7% of test conversations, concentrated right at tool-chain decision points) that `data_validator.py` never catches and that the model was directly trained on under the current loss recipe. I've documented it in `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5, added it as **R12** in CLAUDE.md, and updated project memory.

Two of the three original checks are done (chat-template folding: clean; row-slicing exposure bias: inconclusive/likely not the cause). The `finish_reason` check and the headroom/pass@k re-probes are blocked here — no GPU and no merged weights in this sandbox — and need to run on the actual training host.

I haven't touched the data or written a fix yet, per the Iron Law (root cause first, no fixes without it) — next step would be to fix the corruption (role-enum validator + filter/repair) and re-measure the 0.674 baseline before running the loss-mask/upsampling experiment, since right now that baseline is measured against a validation set that's 12.5% corrupted. Want me to implement that fix?

content: We're improving Cat A tool-calling by fixing SFT data quality before any retrain. Forensics found corrupted training rows contaminating 12.5% of validation data; next step is implementing that data cleanup fix.

content.text:
Here's exactly what I looked at during the forensics pass:

**Training/eval data files (the actual JSONL datasets):**
- `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/train.jsonl` — 4,414 conversations. Main corpus: inspected schema (top-level keys, message roles), scanned all messages for role distribution, ran the malformed-role scan, and the narration/tool-call adjacency scan (check #1).
- `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/validation.jsonl` — 519 conversations. Scanned for the same malformed-role contamination (found 65/519, 12.5%).
- `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/test.jsonl` — 261 conversations. Same scan (20/261, 7.7%).
- `data/output/grpo/task_a/validation.jsonl` (290 rows) and `train.jsonl` (2,502 rows) — read one row to confirm these are full multi-turn conversations (not pre-sliced per-turn rows), which clarified that `training/grpo.py`'s per-turn extraction happens at load time, not at data-generation time.

**Checkpoint artifacts:**
- `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000/` — directory listing (confirmed LoRA adapter only, 75MB, no merged weights).
- Its `chat_template.jinja` — read directly and grepped for role-handling logic; also loaded via `transformers.AutoTokenizer` (which failed on a version-skew error unrelated to the actual template logic, so I inspected the Jinja source directly instead).
- `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` (the frozen config that actually produced this checkpoint) — read in the prior turn, confirmed it predates the `loss_mask` flag.

**Code (to understand how the data gets consumed, not data itself):**
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/data/data_validator.py` — grepped for role validation (found it only checks `messages[0]`).
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/data/chat_template_converter.py` — grepped for role handling.
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/training/sft.py` — grepped for assistant-role checks, `render_response_only_sample`.
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/training/grpo.py` — grepped for the per-turn extraction filter (`_load_grpo_jsonl`, lines ~324/328).
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/data/*.py` (all files) — grepped for `"to="`/`harmony`/channel-routing handling to confirm this corruption pattern is entirely unhandled (it is).

**What I explicitly could not access** (confirmed absent/blocked, not inspected):
- `runs/preflight/heldout_composite_audit_ckpt1000.json` — the actual audit's list of 150 sampled rows + completions. Gitignored, not present on disk (only `runs/preflight/provenance_20260707_040507.txt` exists there).
- GPU/CUDA (`torch.cuda.is_available()` returned `False`, no `nvidia-smi`) — so no generation, no re-running the audit or probes.

content.content:
=== dvc.yaml around line 62 ===
stages:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Task A — Multi-turn workflow conversations
  # See docs/data_generation_recipes.md for full rationale.
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  task_a_benchmark:
    desc: >-
      1 000 placeholder conversations for Phase 1 model ranking.
      No teacher model or API key required.
    cmd: ./scripts/generate_benchmark_data.sh
    deps:
      - scripts/generate_benchmark_data.sh
      - src/llm_workflow_agents/data/generate_workflows.py
      - data/templates/workflow_prompt_template.txt
      - data/templates/tool_schemas_L1_to_L5.json
    params:
      - configs/benchmark/phase1_matrix.yaml:
          - task_a
    outs:
      - data/output/benchmark/task_a
    # Levels L1–L5, 200 samples/level, seed=100, lang=mixed, behavior=default

  task_a_sft:
    desc: >-
      4 450 raw conversations generated for SFT, then cleaned to 4 445.
      Requires OPENAI_API_KEY and GEMINI_API_KEY.
    cmd: >-
      ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh &&
      python scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py
        --input-dir data/output/sft/task_a
        --output-dir data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned
        --quiet
    deps:
      - scripts/generate_sft_data.sh
      - scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py
      - src/llm_workflow_agents/data/generate_workflows.py
      - data/templates/workflow_prompt_template.txt
      - data/templates/tool_schemas_L1_to_L5.json
    outs:
      - data/output/sft/task_a
      - data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned
    # Levels L1–L5, seed=42, behavior=adversarial (87.5%) + cooperative_only (12.5%)
    # Legs: en/gpt-5.4-mini, th/gemini-3-flash-preview, code_switch/gpt-5.4-nano
    # Cleanup: drops 5 truncated rows, strips 264 role-confused tool messages,
    #          flags 476 empty-terminal convs with terminal_reached=false

  task_a_sft_splits:
    desc: >-
      Deterministic 85/10/5 train/validation/test split of the cleaned Task A
      SFT corpus. Seed=42. Reused across SFT training, GRPO training, and
      held-out evaluation.
    cmd: >-
      python scripts/split_task_a_sft.py
        --input-dir data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned
        --output-dir data/output/sft/task_a_splits
        --force
    deps:
      - scripts/split_task_a_sft.py
      - data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned
    outs:
      - data/output/sft/task_a_splits

  task_a_grpo:
    desc: >-
      GRPO prompts: L3–L5 subset of the cleaned SFT splits (no new generation).
      Rewards are recomputed online from policy generations, so GRPO needs
      prompts — not labels. See docs/data_generation_recipes.md.
    cmd: >-
      python scripts/filter_grpo_data.py
        --input-dir data/output/sft/task_a_splits
        --output-dir data/output/grpo/task_a
    deps:
      - scripts/filter_grpo_data.py
      - data/output/sft/task_a_splits
    outs:
      - data/output/grpo/task_a
    # Filter only — L3-L5 of train.jsonl + validation.jsonl. test.jsonl is
    # reserved for final evaluation and is intentionally not included.

  task_a_eval:
    desc: >-
      500 validation + 500 test held-out conversations.
      Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.
    cmd: ./scripts/generate_eval_data.sh
    deps:
      - scripts/generate_eval_data.sh
      - src/llm_workflow_agents/data/generate_workflows.py
      - data/templates/workflow_prompt_template.txt
      - data/templates/tool_schemas_L1_to_L5.json
    outs:
      - data/output/val/task_a    # seed=300
      - data/output/test/task_a   # seed=400
    # Levels L1–L5, 100 samples/level/split, lang=mixed, behavior=default
    # test split must NOT be used during training or hyperparameter selection

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Phase 2 — SFT fine-tuning
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  task_a_sft_gemma4_26b_a4b:
    desc: >-
      Phase 2 SFT — Gemma-4 26B-A4B-it on cleaned Task A data. Outputs
      LoRA adapter checkpoints at every save_steps interval plus the
      train log under checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/.

      Training takes several hours on an H100. To register an existing
      checkpoint without re-running training, use:
          dvc commit task_a_sft_gemma4_26b_a4b
      Push to the GCS remote afterwards:
          dvc push task_a_sft_gemma4_26b_a4b
    cmd: >-
      ./scripts/run_phase2_sft.sh
      --model-config configs/models_exp_a/gemma4_26b_a4b.yaml
      --sft-config configs/training/sft_cat_a.yaml
    deps:
      - scripts/run_phase2_sft.sh
      - src/llm_workflow_agents/training/sft.py
      - src/llm_workflow_agents/training/lora_targets.py
      - configs/models_exp_a/gemma4_26b_a4b.yaml

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Clean Task A SFT JSONL data before fine-tuning.

Three fixes applied in a single pass:
  1. Drop truncated rows (no non-system turns).
  2. Drop role-confused tool messages (role:"tool" with <tool_call> content).
  3. Flag empty terminal_state as ground_truth.terminal_reached=false so the
     GRPO reward function can skip the completion sub-reward on those rows
     rather than silently scoring them 0.

Usage:
    python scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py \\
        --input-dir data/output/sft/task_a \\
        --output-dir data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned

    # Inspect counts without writing output:
    python scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py \\
        --input-dir data/output/sft/task_a \\
        --output-dir data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned \\
        --dry-run
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def clean_record(record: dict) -> tuple[dict | None, str | None]:
    """Return (cleaned_record, drop_reason).

    drop_reason is None when the record is kept (possibly modified).
    """
    msgs = record.get("messages", [])

    # 1. Drop truncated rows (system-only conversations)
    if not any(m.get("role") != "system" for m in msgs):
        return None, "truncated_no_non_system_turns"

    # 2. Strip role-confused tool messages (content may be str or dict/list)
    def _is_role_confused(m: dict) -> bool:
        if m.get("role") != "tool":
            return False
        content = m.get("content")
        return isinstance(content, str) and content.strip().startswith("<tool_call>")

    cleaned_msgs = [m for m in msgs if not _is_role_confused(m)]
    record["messages"] = cleaned_msgs

    # Re-check: if stripping left only a system message, drop the row
    if not any(m.get("role") != "system" for m in cleaned_msgs):
        return None, "truncated_after_role_confusion_filter"

    # 3. Flag empty terminal_state
    gt = record.setdefault("ground_truth", {})
    ts = gt.get("terminal_state")
    gt["terminal_reached"] = bool(ts)

    return record, None


def _clean_file(
    src: Path,
    dst: Path,
    dry_run: bool,
) -> tuple[int, int, int, int, int]:
    """Clean a single JSONL file.

    Returns (total, dropped_truncated, dropped_role_confused_convs,
             stripped_rc_messages, flagged_terminal).
    """
    total = dropped_truncated = dropped_rc_convs = stripped_rc_msgs = flagged_terminal = 0
    out_lines: list[str] = []

    with open(src) as fh:
        for lineno, line in enumerate(fh, 1):
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                record = json.loads(line)
            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
                sys.exit(f"Error: {src}:{lineno}: {exc}")

            total += 1
            before_len = len(record.get("messages", []))
            cleaned, reason = clean_record(record)

            if cleaned is None:
                if reason == "truncated_no_non_system_turns":
                    dropped_truncated += 1
                else:
                    dropped_rc_convs += 1
                continue

            after_len = len(cleaned.get("messages", []))
            stripped_rc_msgs += before_len - after_len

            if not cleaned.get("ground_truth", {}).get("terminal_reached", True):
                flagged_terminal += 1

            out_lines.append(json.dumps(cleaned, ensure_ascii=False))

    if not dry_run:
        dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(dst, "w") as fh:
            fh.write("\n".join(out_lines))
            if out_lines:
                fh.write("\n")

    return total, dropped_truncated, dropped_rc_convs, stripped_rc_msgs, flagged_terminal


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Clean Task A SFT JSONL files before fine-tuning."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--input-dir",
        required=True,
        metavar="DIR",
        help="Directory containing raw *.jsonl files.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output-dir",
        required=True,
        metavar="DIR",
        help="Destination directory for cleaned *.jsonl files.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="Report counts without writing any files.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "-q", "--quiet",
        action="store_true",
        help="Suppress per-file progress output.",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    input_dir = Path(args.input_dir)
    output_dir = Path(args.output_dir)

    if not input_dir.is_dir():
        sys.exit(f"Error: input directory not found: {input_dir}")

    src_files = sorted(input_dir.glob("*.jsonl"))
    if not src_files:
        sys.exit(f"Error: no *.jsonl files found in {input_dir}")

    if args.dry_run:
        print("[dry-run] No files will be written.")

    grand_total = grand_kept = grand_trunc = grand_rc = grand_rc_msgs = grand_flag = 0

    for src in src_files:
        dst = output_dir / src.name
        total, trunc, rc, rc_msgs, flag = _clean_file(src, dst, dry_run=args.dry_run)
        kept = total - trunc - rc
        grand_total += total
        grand_kept += kept
        grand_trunc += trunc
        grand_rc += rc
        grand_rc_msgs += rc_msgs
        grand_flag += flag

        if not args.quiet:
            parts = [f"{src.name}: {total} in → {kept} kept"]
            if trunc:
                parts.append(f"{trunc} truncated dropped")
            if rc:
                parts.append(f"{rc} role-confused-conv dropped")
            if rc_msgs:
                parts.append(f"{rc_msgs} role-confused msgs stripped")
            if flag:
                parts.append(f"{flag} terminal_reached=False flagged")
            print("  " + ", ".join(parts))

    print()
    print("=" * 60)
    print(f"Input files    : {len(src_files)}")
    print(f"Total records  : {grand_total}")
    print(f"Kept           : {grand_kept}")
    print(f"  Dropped (truncated)                : {grand_trunc}")
    print(f"  Dropped (role-conf conv, all-bad)  : {grand_rc}")
    print(f"  Stripped role-confused tool msgs   : {grand_rc_msgs}")
    print(f"  Flagged terminal_reached=False     : {grand_flag}")

    if args.dry_run:
        print("\n[dry-run] No files written.")
    else:
        print(f"\nCleaned files written to: {output_dir}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
Good catch — that changed the picture significantly. Here's what I found:

**`data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/` (what I originally inspected) is not part of the DVC pipeline at all.** Checking `dvc.yaml`: the `task_a_sft` stage's declared output is `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned` (no `splits/` subdirectory), and the actual canonical split directory is produced by a separate `task_a_sft_splits` stage into `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`. The `splits/` folder nested inside `task_a_cleaned/` isn't declared as an output anywhere, isn't tracked by git or DVC (it's covered by the `/task_a_cleaned` gitignore pattern), and is dated 2026-05-13 — while the canonical `task_a_splits` is dated 2026-07-07 and has **roughly double the rows** (10,743 vs 5,194 total). It looks like an orphaned snapshot from before the pipeline had a dedicated splitting stage.

The reason I looked there rather than at `task_a_splits`: the **frozen config that actually trained checkpoint-1000** (`.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`) points to `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits`, not the canonical path — so for auditing *that specific checkpoint*, it was the right directory. But that's itself worth flagging: **ckpt-1000 was trained on a non-DVC-tracked, non-reproducible data snapshot**, separate from the role-corruption bug.

**More importantly, checking the canonical `task_a_splits` and the downstream GRPO data closes a gap in my earlier conclusion:**

| Dataset | Train | Validation | Test |
|---|---|---|---|
| `task_a_cleaned/splits` (stale, what ckpt-1000 trained on) | 3.8% | 12.5% | 7.7% |
| `task_a_splits` (canonical, current) | 5.1% | 5.7% | 4.6% |
| `data/output/grpo/task_a` (what the held-out audit actually draws from) | 17.2% | **22.4%** | — |

The corruption is **not** an artifact of the stale snapshot — it's present in the current canonical pipeline output too, and it's substantially *worse* in the GRPO/held-out data (nearly a quarter of validation conversations), because that stage filters to L3–L5 complexity only, and more complex conversations hit the underlying teacher-model failure more often.

**Root cause is now precise.** I read `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`. Its "strips 264 role-confused tool messages" (from the `dvc.yaml` description) refers to a *different* bug entirely: `_is_role_confused()` only strips messages where `role == "tool"` and the content *starts with* `<tool_call>` text. It has no logic at all for messages whose `role` *field itself* is corrupted (`"assistant to=tool"`, etc.) — those pass straight through untouched. `scripts/filter_grpo_data.py` has no role-handling logic either. So the bug isn't caught anywhere in the pipeline, at any stage.

I've updated the doc, CLAUDE.md, and memory to correct this.

**This is a real, independent bug** — worth fixing (filter/repair at the data layer, add a role-enum

before any SFT retrain, since it currently contaminates ~4-12% of every split including 12.5% of
the held-out validation set the audit's own baseline was measured against.
input.new_string:
**This is a real, independent bug** — worth fixing (filter/repair at the data layer, add a role-enum
check to `data_validator.py`) regardless of how the loss-mask/upsampling questions resolve, and
before any SFT retrain, since it currently contaminates ~4-12% of every split including 12.5% of
the held-out validation set the audit's own baseline was measured against.

**Correction (2026-07-14, prompted by a user question):** `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/`
(inspected above) is **not part of the DVC pipeline** — `dvc.yaml`'s `task_a_sft` stage only
declares `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned` as an output (no `splits/` subdir); the canonical,
DVC-tracked split directory is produced by a separate `task_a_sft_splits` stage into
`data/output/sft/task_a_splits`. The `task_a_cleaned/splits/` directory is untracked by git/DVC
(covered by the `/task_a_cleaned` gitignore pattern), dated 2026-05-13, and roughly half the size
of the current canonical `task_a_splits` (dated 2026-07-07, ~2× the rows). It was the right
directory to inspect for *this specific checkpoint* — `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`, the frozen
config that actually trained ckpt-1000, points to it — but that itself means **ckpt-1000 was
trained on a non-reproducible, out-of-pipeline data snapshot**, a separate issue from the role
corruption.

Checking further confirms the corruption is **not** an artifact of that stale snapshot — it's in
the current canonical pipeline output too, and worse in the data the held-out audit actually draws
from:

| Dataset | Train | Validation | Test |
|---|---|---|---|
| `task_a_cleaned/splits` (stale) | 167/4414 (3.8%) | 65/519 (12.5%) | 20/261 (7.7%) |
| `task_a_splits` (canonical) | 465/9131 (5.1%) | 61/1074 (5.7%) | 25/538 (4.6%) |
| `data/output/grpo/task_a` (audit's actual source, L3-L5 filtered) | 431/2502 (17.2%) | **65/290 (22.4%)** | — |

Root cause pinned down precisely by reading `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`: the "264 role-confused
tool messages" the `dvc.yaml` stage description advertises stripping is a **different** bug —
`_is_role_confused()` only strips messages where `role == "tool"` **and** content starts with
`<tool_call>` text. It has no logic for messages whose `role` field itself is corrupted (e.g.
`"assistant to=tool"`) — those pass straight through untouched, at every stage
(`clean_task_a_sft.py` → `split_task_a_sft.py` → `filter_grpo_data.py`, none of which validate the
role enum). Fixing this means patching `clean_task_a_sft.py`'s `clean_record()` to also drop/strip
any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-running the DVC pipeline
(`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) to regenerate everything downstream — not
patching a static snapshot file.

input.old_string: - R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/{train,validation,test}.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (2,143 messages across 167/4414 train (3.8%), 65/519 validation (12.5%), 20/261 test (7.7%) conversations) — likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`, so this is invisible to existing validation. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: add a role-enum check to `data_validator.py` and filter/repair affected rows before any further Cat A SFT retrain — this contaminates 12.5% of the validation split the 0.674 held-out baseline was measured against.
input.new_string: - R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks), present at every stage of the Task A pipeline: `data/output/sft/task_a_splits` (canonical, DVC-tracked: 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test conversations affected) and worse in `data/output/grpo/task_a` (L3-L5 filtered, what the held-out audit draws from: 17.2% train / **22.4% validation**). 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. Root cause pinned to `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record`'s `_is_role_confused()`, which only strips `tool`-role messages containing literal `<tool_call>` text — a different bug — and has no logic for a corrupted `role` field itself; `scripts/split_task_a_sft.py` and `scripts/filter_grpo_data.py` don't validate roles either, so it passes through every stage untouched. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. Separately: checkpoint-1000 (the checkpoint this investigation centers on) was trained via `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`, which points to `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` — an untracked, non-canonical, non-reproducible snapshot (not a declared DVC output; superseded by the current `task_a_splits`) — a separate reproducibility gap worth closing before the next training run.

lines: - R8: Gemma4 + TurboQuant → **works with perf caveat**. Two hooks in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py`: (1) `_install_gemma4_mixed_backend_hook` bypasses the TRITON_ATTN global force at `vllm/model_executor/models/config.py:100`; (2) `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` auto-injects `enforce_eager=True` for Gemma-4 + `turboquant_*`, skipping vLLM v1's broken mixed-KV profiler (`gpu_model_runner.py:6598`). CUDA graphs off → expect ~15-25% decode throughput penalty vs graph-mode. Numerical correctness unaffected. Restore graph-mode once upstream lands a per-layer-aware profiler.

- R9: Gemma-4 (26B-A4B, 31B) + Unsloth `fast_inference=True` → **architecture allowlist rejection**. Unsloth 2026.5.2's `VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at `unsloth/models/vision.py:242` is hardcoded to `{qwen2_5_vl, gemma3, mistral3, qwen3_vl, qwen3_vl_moe}`. Gemma-4 ships as a SigLIP+Gemma4 multimodal stack, so `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(..., fast_inference=True)` raises `RuntimeError: Found architectures: siglip, gemma4!` (`unsloth/models/vision.py:610`). **Auto-resolved.** `training/grpo.py::_detect_model_family` reads the SFT checkpoint's `adapter_config.json → base_model_name_or_path` and resolves `AutoConfig.model_type`. When the family is in `UNSLOTH_VLLM_INCOMPATIBLE_FAMILIES = {"gemma4"}`, `use_vllm` is forced to `False` with a `vllm_rollout_disabled_unsloth_incompat` warning; training proceeds with HF `model.generate()` rollouts (~31 s/step on H100 80GB → ~8.6 h per 1000-step GRPO run). Other Cat A models (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Mistral-Small-3.1-24B, Qwen3.6 variants) are unaffected — they continue to get Unsloth colocate vLLM rollouts. To re-enable vLLM rollouts on Gemma-4: (a) wait for Unsloth to add `gemma4` to the allowlist (file an issue at github.com/unslothai/unsloth), (b) switch to external vLLM server mode (`vllm_mode="server"` + a separate `trl vllm-serve` process — tight on 80GB but viable with FP8 weights), or (c) monkey-patch `unsloth.models.vision.VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at import time (quick to try, may fail deeper in vLLM-engine init).

- R10: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B + vLLM → **no tagged release supports it** (verified 2026-06-11: vLLM 0.22.1 model registry has no `diffusion_gemma` entry; transformers added the model defs in 5.11.0; the HF checkpoints ship no remote code). Serving works ONLY via vLLM's `dgemma` branch (PR #45163, open) — prebuilt images `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma-cu129` (CUDA 12.9) / `:gemma` (CUDA 13.0, driver ≥ 580). Required flags per the RedHatAI model card: `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--max-num-seqs 4`, `--hf-overrides '{"diffusion_sampler": "entropy_bound", "diffusion_entropy_bound": 0.1}'`. Wired into `deployments/models/` (`VLLM_IMAGE` + `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER` + `VLLM_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE` knobs; preset in `.env.example`). `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) cannot serve it — fails at ModelConfig validation ("model type `diffusion_gemma` ... not recognize this architecture"). Keep `kv-cache-dtype` blank (fp8 KV on the diffusion decode path unvalidated). Re-pin to a tagged image once PR #45163 merges into a release.

- R11: Gemma-4 12B (`gemma4_unified`) + vLLM → **needs vLLM ≥ v0.23.0** (verified 2026-07-06 against the model registry: `Gemma4Unified*` absent through v0.22.1, present in v0.23.0/v0.24.0). The pinned serving image (`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0`) and `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) both fail at ModelConfig validation ("model type `gemma4_unified` ... does not recognize this architecture"). On L4 24GB the BF16 checkpoint can never fit regardless (11.96B params ≈ 23.9 GB weights > 22.03 GiB usable) — use `RedHatAI/gemma-4-12B-it-FP8-Dynamic` (~12.75 GB load, compressed-tensors FP8, tested by RedHat against vLLM nightly) with `VLLM_IMAGE=vllm/vllm-openai:v0.24.0`. Verified serving on L4 (2026-07-06): fp8 KV, `--max-num-batched-tokens 4096`, 50,087-token KV cache at 8K max_model_len; tool calling via parser `gemma4` works; also verified with LMCache + `--enforce-eager`. Preset in `.env.example`.

**How to apply:** fix this (add a role-enum validator check + filter/repair affected rows) before
any Cat A SFT retrain or before trusting the 0.674 held-out baseline — it's measured against a
validation split that's 12.5% contaminated. This is now the top-priority action, ahead of the
loss-mask A/B and upsampling experiments. Checks #2 (finish_reason on the actual audit rows) and
the headroom/pass@k re-probes still need the real GPU host — this session had no CUDA and no
merged weights, only the LoRA adapter.

**Correction (2026-07-14, user caught this):** `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/` — the
directory the forensics above ran against — is **not the canonical dataset**. It's untracked by
git/DVC (not a declared `dvc.yaml` output), and is what the *frozen config that trained
checkpoint-1000* (`.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`) happens to point to — a separate
reproducibility gap. The canonical, DVC-pipeline dataset is `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`
(stage `task_a_sft_splits`). Checked it directly: the role-corruption bug is present there too
(5.1%/5.7%/4.6% train/val/test), and *worse* in `data/output/grpo/task_a` — the L3-L5-filtered
data the held-out audit actually draws from — at **17.2% train / 22.4% validation**. Root cause
confirmed by reading `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`: its "role-confused" cleaning only strips
`tool`-role messages containing literal `<tool_call>` text (a different bug); it has no check for
a corrupted `role` field itself, and neither does `split_task_a_sft.py` or `filter_grpo_data.py` —
so it's an active, unpatched pipeline bug, not stale-data noise. Full detail:
`docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1 (correction inline) and CLAUDE.md R12.
**How to apply:** the fix belongs in `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` (add a role-enum
check), followed by re-running the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` →
`task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to any single snapshot file. Also flag to the user: retraining
should target the canonical `task_a_splits`-derived pipeline, not the stale `task_a_cleaned/splits`
snapshot ckpt-1000 used.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: project-grpo-cat-a-stall
description: Phase 2 Cat A GRPO (Gemma-4-26B-A4B) fine-tuning is stalled at an unresolved maintainer decision fork as of 2026-07-14
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 82ed40c5-aaa3-46c6-8fc8-378f94366a67
---

Single-turn online GRPO on Gemma-4-26B-A4B Cat A does not work, and the trajectory-reward
pivot tried as a fallback also came back MARGINAL on every cheap knob. See
`docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md` and `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` for the
full record.

**Why:** Reward lattice is too coarse — most 8-sample GRPO groups collapse to a single reward
rung, so the advantage (and gradient) is exactly zero on ~70%+ of groups. A headroom probe
(2026-07-09), a trajectory-variance probe re-run at n=150 (2026-07-10), and a final T=0.8→1.0
knob (most recent commit, `d9d8b44`/`4c7f89d`) all landed MARGINAL, never clearing GO. The
SFT-only ceiling check was also checked as an alternative — after fixing a scoring-granularity
bug (per-turn-fair composite + GT sanitizer for fabricated tool args), the honest baseline is
0.674 composite, still under the 0.75 target.

**How to apply:** The trajectory track's cheap knobs are now exhausted per the recorded
2026-07-10 routing decision. Three paths remain, and choosing between them is an explicitly
**deferred maintainer decision**, not yet made as of the last commit:
1. Fall back to single-turn RFT (rejection-sampling fine-tuning, Path #1 in the viability memo).
2. Ship SFT-only for Phase 2 Cat A (accept the 0.674 ceiling).
3. Invest in full trainer wiring for the trajectory reward despite it not clearing GO.

When asked about Phase 2 / GRPO status, don't say "complete" — say this fork is open and ask
which path the user wants before doing further GRPO work. Verify against `git log` for newer
commits before relying on this, since this is a fast-moving track.

**Update (2026-07-14) — revised action plan after a Fable review of SFT-improvement options.**
The genuine (non-artifact) weakness is "announce-but-don't-call": on tool-expected held-out rows
the model narrates intent/state correctly but under-emits `<tool_call>`. First hypothesis was
that `checkpoint-1000` trained with `loss_mask=all_tokens` (gradient diluted across boilerplate
system prompt) instead of the already-implemented-but-never-used `response_only` recipe. Fable's
review found this plausible but unproven, and surfaced a real confound: ckpt-1000 trained
**before** the GT sanitizer (which drops 32 fabricated-arg tool calls) existed, so any retrain
changes both loss-recipe and data at once unless controlled. Revised, higher-priority plan before
spending GPU on a retrain:
1. Cheap forensics first: inspect rendered training rows for tool-expected turns (single-turn
   slicing may have literally created announce-only gold rows), check `finish_reason`/truncation
   on the 15 announce-but-don't-call rows, verify Gemma chat-template system-prompt folding.
2. Run the RFT headroom re-probe (already flagged as overdue in
   `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` §6) **now**, in parallel — it's nearly free and
   gates whether RFT is even viable regardless of SFT changes. Add a pass@k (T≈1.0) probe on the
   52 tool-expected held-out rows specifically — the real RFT-feasibility number, not yet measured.
3. If a masking/data experiment is still warranted, combine `response_only` + tool-turn upsampling
   into one factorial run on sanitized data rather than two sequential single-variable retrains.
4. Known measurement gaps to fix before trusting any delta: n=52 tool-expected rows → ~±13pp CI,
   too small to trust deltas under ~15pp; the 0.674 composite is on a different metric than the
   0.75 target it's being compared to (target needs re-deriving for the per-turn-fair scorer).

**How to apply:** don't recommend a straight SFT retrain on this checkpoint without first doing
the forensics above — the loss-mask fix is unverified and a confounded retrain would waste GPU
time without attributable signal.

**Forensics result (2026-07-14) — found a real, independent data-corruption bug (CLAUDE.md R12).**
`data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits/*.jsonl` contains messages with malformed roles like
`assistant to=tool` and empty content — 2,143 messages, contaminating 3.8% of train / **12.5% of
validation** / 7.7% of test conversations. 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message
(exactly where the model must decide to narrate vs. call the next tool). `data_validator.py`
never validates the role enum past `messages[0]`, so this passed cleaning untouched. Gemma-4's
chat template doesn't reject unknown roles — it renders them literally — so under
`loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trained with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty
turns. Full detail in `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1.
**How to apply:** fix this (add a role-enum validator check + filter/repair affected rows) before
any Cat A SFT retrain or before trusting the 0.674 held-out baseline — it's measured against a
validation split that's 12.5% contaminated. This is now the top-priority action, ahead of the
loss-mask A/B and upsampling experiments. Checks #2 (finish_reason on the actual audit rows) and
the headroom/pass@k re-probes still need the real GPU host — this session had no CUDA and no
merged weights, only the LoRA adapter.

the headroom/pass@k re-probes still need the real GPU host — this session had no CUDA and no
merged weights, only the LoRA adapter.

content.text: **Summary of the correction:** the data I originally inspected was the right one for auditing checkpoint-1000 specifically (its frozen config points there), but it's an untracked, non-canonical snapshot — not what `dvc.yaml`'s actual pipeline produces. Checking the real canonical data (`task_a_splits`) and what the held-out audit actually draws from (`data/output/grpo/task_a`) shows the bug is worse than I first reported — **22.4% of the GRPO/held-out validation conversations are affected**, and it's a live, unpatched gap in `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`, not stale-data noise. The real fix is at that script (plus re-running the DVC pipeline), not a one-off data patch — I've corrected the doc, CLAUDE.md, and memory accordingly.

aiTitle: Review current project status from commits

content: We're improving Cat A tool-calling by finding why the model skips tool calls; forensics found a real data-cleaning bug affecting up to 22% of validation data. Next: patch clean_task_a_sft.py and rerun the pipeline.

content.text: Let me verify precisely rather than just agree — I want to check whether the two datasets are actually different generations (different conversations) or the same corpus re-split, and pin down the timeline.

echo "=== data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/ contents (the actual dvc.yaml-declared output, parent of the orphaned splits/ dir) ==="

find data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned -maxdepth 1 -iname '*.jsonl' | xargs -I{} wc -l {} 2>/dev/null | tail -5
echo

git log --oneline --follow -- dvc.yaml | tail -20
echo

git log -S"task_a_sft_splits" --oneline -- dvc.yaml

content.text:
Good instinct to press on this — I dug into the actual training log rather than trusting the frozen config file, and the answer is more interesting than "yes."

**The checkpoint was NOT trained on the wrong dataset — it was trained on the correct canonical one.** `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log` shows the actual data loaded at training time:

```
2026-07-02 04:57:53 [info] sft_dataset_render  loss_mask=all_tokens
Map: 100%|██████████| 9131/9131  [train]
Map: 100%|██████████| 1074/1074  [eval]
```

**9,131 train / 1,074 eval — that's an exact match for the canonical `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`, not the stale `task_a_cleaned/splits` (4,414/519) I originally inspected.**

So the real bug I found is different from what I told you: **`.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` — the "frozen" config file that's supposed to be the reproducibility record of this training run — has a wrong `data.source` field.** It says `.../task_a_cleaned/splits`, but that's not what actually got loaded. That's a provenance/metadata bug in the project's run-freezing mechanism, separate from the data-corruption bug — the record of what trained this checkpoint is itself unreliable.

Net effect on everything else:
- The role-corruption finding still holds, but the **correct contamination rate for what ckpt-1000 actually trained on is 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test** (canonical `task_a_splits`'s numbers), not the 3.8%/12.5%/7.7% I originally reported from the stale directory.
- The held-out audit's data source (`data/output/grpo/task_a`, derived from canonical `task_a_splits` per `dvc.yaml`) is unaffected — that 17.2%/22.4% figure was already correct.

Fixing this doc trail now.

input.old_string:
Root cause pinned down precisely by reading `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`: the "264 role-confused
tool messages" the `dvc.yaml` stage description advertises stripping is a **different** bug —
`_is_role_confused()` only strips messages where `role == "tool"` **and** content starts with
`<tool_call>` text. It has no logic for messages whose `role` field itself is corrupted (e.g.
`"assistant to=tool"`) — those pass straight through untouched, at every stage
(`clean_task_a_sft.py` → `split_task_a_sft.py` → `filter_grpo_data.py`, none of which validate the
role enum). Fixing this means patching `clean_task_a_sft.py`'s `clean_record()` to also drop/strip
any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-running the DVC pipeline
(`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) to regenerate everything downstream — not
patching a static snapshot file.
input.new_string:
Root cause pinned down precisely by reading `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py`: the "264 role-confused
tool messages" the `dvc.yaml` stage description advertises stripping is a **different** bug —
`_is_role_confused()` only strips messages where `role == "tool"` **and** content starts with
`<tool_call>` text. It has no logic for messages whose `role` field itself is corrupted (e.g.
`"assistant to=tool"`) — those pass straight through untouched, at every stage
(`clean_task_a_sft.py` → `split_task_a_sft.py` → `filter_grpo_data.py`, none of which validate the
role enum). Fixing this means patching `clean_task_a_sft.py`'s `clean_record()` to also drop/strip
any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-running the DVC pipeline
(`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) to regenerate everything downstream — not
patching a static snapshot file.

**Second correction (2026-07-14, same follow-up):** checked `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log`
directly rather than trusting `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`'s `data.source` field. The log's
actual dataset-mapping counts are unambiguous:

```
2026-07-02 04:57:53 [info] sft_dataset_render  loss_mask=all_tokens
Map: 100%|██████████| 9131/9131  [train]
Map: 100%|██████████| 1074/1074  [eval]
```

**9,131 train / 1,074 eval exactly matches the canonical `task_a_splits`, not the stale
`task_a_cleaned/splits` (4,414/519) the frozen config claims.** So ckpt-1000 was **not** trained on
the wrong/orphaned snapshot — it trained on the correct, canonical, DVC-tracked data. The actual
bug is that **`.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`'s `data.source` field is itself wrong** — a
provenance/reproducibility bug in whatever mechanism froze that config, independent of the data-
corruption bug in §5.1. Two separate, now-fixed-in-understanding issues:

1. **Provenance bug**: the frozen run config for ckpt-1000 does not accurately record what data was
   used. Needs its own fix (regenerate/correct the frozen snapshot mechanism) before trusting any
   future frozen config as ground truth for "what did this checkpoint train on."
2. **Data-corruption bug (§5.1)** stands, but the correct contamination rate for what ckpt-1000
   *actually* trained on is the canonical `task_a_splits` numbers — **5.1% train / 5.7% validation
   / 4.6% test** — not the 3.8%/12.5%/7.7% originally reported from the stale directory. The held-out
   audit's own data source (`data/output/grpo/task_a`, 17.2%/22.4%) is unaffected by this correction,
   since it was already derived from the canonical `task_a_splits`.

aiTitle: Review current project status from commits

input.old_string: Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. Separately: checkpoint-1000 (the checkpoint this investigation centers on) was trained via `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`, which points to `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` — an untracked, non-canonical, non-reproducible snapshot (not a declared DVC output; superseded by the current `task_a_splits`) — a separate reproducibility gap worth closing before the next training run.
input.new_string:
Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. The contamination rate for what checkpoint-1000 actually trained on (see below) is 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test.
- R12b: `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` provenance bug — the frozen config recorded for checkpoint-1000 claims `data.source: data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` (an untracked, non-canonical, half-sized snapshot; not a declared DVC output), but `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log` proves the actual run loaded 9,131 train / 1,074 eval examples — an exact match for the canonical, DVC-tracked `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`, not the 4,414/519 the frozen config implies. So checkpoint-1000 **was** trained on the correct canonical dataset; the frozen run-config snapshot mechanism itself is unreliable and should not be trusted as ground truth for "what data trained this checkpoint" without cross-checking `train.log`. Discovered 2026-07-14 (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1 second correction). Fix: investigate why the frozen config's `data.source` doesn't match the resolved path actually passed to the trainer (e.g. a default-path fallback in `training/sft.py`, or the freezing step capturing the wrong config file) before relying on any `.runs/*/*.yaml` file for provenance.

- R12b: `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` provenance bug — the frozen config recorded for checkpoint-1000 claims `data.source: data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` (an untracked, non-canonical, half-sized snapshot; not a declared DVC output), but `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log` proves the actual run loaded 9,131 train / 1,074 eval examples — an exact match for the canonical, DVC-tracked `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`, not the 4,414/519 the frozen config implies. So checkpoint-1000 **was** trained on the correct canonical dataset; the frozen run-config snapshot mechanism itself is unreliable and should not be trusted as ground truth for "what data trained this checkpoint" without cross-checking `train.log`. Discovered 2026-07-14 (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1 second correction). Fix: investigate why the frozen config's `data.source` doesn't match the resolved path actually passed to the trainer (e.g. a default-path fallback in `training/sft.py`, or the freezing step capturing the wrong config file) before relying on any `.runs/*/*.yaml` file for provenance.

lines: - R8: Gemma4 + TurboQuant → **works with perf caveat**. Two hooks in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py`: (1) `_install_gemma4_mixed_backend_hook` bypasses the TRITON_ATTN global force at `vllm/model_executor/models/config.py:100`; (2) `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` auto-injects `enforce_eager=True` for Gemma-4 + `turboquant_*`, skipping vLLM v1's broken mixed-KV profiler (`gpu_model_runner.py:6598`). CUDA graphs off → expect ~15-25% decode throughput penalty vs graph-mode. Numerical correctness unaffected. Restore graph-mode once upstream lands a per-layer-aware profiler.

- R9: Gemma-4 (26B-A4B, 31B) + Unsloth `fast_inference=True` → **architecture allowlist rejection**. Unsloth 2026.5.2's `VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at `unsloth/models/vision.py:242` is hardcoded to `{qwen2_5_vl, gemma3, mistral3, qwen3_vl, qwen3_vl_moe}`. Gemma-4 ships as a SigLIP+Gemma4 multimodal stack, so `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(..., fast_inference=True)` raises `RuntimeError: Found architectures: siglip, gemma4!` (`unsloth/models/vision.py:610`). **Auto-resolved.** `training/grpo.py::_detect_model_family` reads the SFT checkpoint's `adapter_config.json → base_model_name_or_path` and resolves `AutoConfig.model_type`. When the family is in `UNSLOTH_VLLM_INCOMPATIBLE_FAMILIES = {"gemma4"}`, `use_vllm` is forced to `False` with a `vllm_rollout_disabled_unsloth_incompat` warning; training proceeds with HF `model.generate()` rollouts (~31 s/step on H100 80GB → ~8.6 h per 1000-step GRPO run). Other Cat A models (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Mistral-Small-3.1-24B, Qwen3.6 variants) are unaffected — they continue to get Unsloth colocate vLLM rollouts. To re-enable vLLM rollouts on Gemma-4: (a) wait for Unsloth to add `gemma4` to the allowlist (file an issue at github.com/unslothai/unsloth), (b) switch to external vLLM server mode (`vllm_mode="server"` + a separate `trl vllm-serve` process — tight on 80GB but viable with FP8 weights), or (c) monkey-patch `unsloth.models.vision.VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at import time (quick to try, may fail deeper in vLLM-engine init).

- R10: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B + vLLM → **no tagged release supports it** (verified 2026-06-11: vLLM 0.22.1 model registry has no `diffusion_gemma` entry; transformers added the model defs in 5.11.0; the HF checkpoints ship no remote code). Serving works ONLY via vLLM's `dgemma` branch (PR #45163, open) — prebuilt images `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma-cu129` (CUDA 12.9) / `:gemma` (CUDA 13.0, driver ≥ 580). Required flags per the RedHatAI model card: `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--max-num-seqs 4`, `--hf-overrides '{"diffusion_sampler": "entropy_bound", "diffusion_entropy_bound": 0.1}'`. Wired into `deployments/models/` (`VLLM_IMAGE` + `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER` + `VLLM_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE` knobs; preset in `.env.example`). `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) cannot serve it — fails at ModelConfig validation ("model type `diffusion_gemma` ... not recognize this architecture"). Keep `kv-cache-dtype` blank (fp8 KV on the diffusion decode path unvalidated). Re-pin to a tagged image once PR #45163 merges into a release.

-- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks), present at every stage of the Task A pipeline: `data/output/sft/task_a_splits` (canonical, DVC-tracked: 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test conversations affected) and worse in `data/output/grpo/task_a` (L3-L5 filtered, what the held-out audit draws from: 17.2% train / **22.4% validation**). 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. Root cause pinned to `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record`'s `_is_role_confused()`, which only strips `tool`-role messages containing literal `<tool_call>` text — a different bug — and has no logic for a corrupted `role` field itself; `scripts/split_task_a_sft.py` and `scripts/filter_grpo_data.py` don't validate roles either, so it passes through every stage untouched. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. Separately: checkpoint-1000 (the checkpoint this investigation centers on) was trained via `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml`, which points to `data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` — an untracked, non-canonical, non-reproducible snapshot (not a declared DVC output; superseded by the current `task_a_splits`) — a separate reproducibility gap worth closing before the next training run.

+- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks), present at every stage of the Task A pipeline: `data/output/sft/task_a_splits` (canonical, DVC-tracked: 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test conversations affected) and worse in `data/output/grpo/task_a` (L3-L5 filtered, what the held-out audit draws from: 17.2% train / **22.4% validation**). 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. The contamination rate for what checkpoint-1000 actually trained on (see below) is 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test.

+- R12b: `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` provenance bug — the frozen config recorded for checkpoint-1000 claims `data.source: data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` (an untracked, non-canonical, half-sized snapshot; not a declared DVC output), but `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log` proves the actual run loaded 9,131 train / 1,074 eval examples — an exact match for the canonical, DVC-tracked `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`, not the 4,414/519 the frozen config implies. Fix: investigate why the frozen config's `data.source` doesn't match the resolved path actually passed to the trainer (e.g. a default-path fallback in `training/sft.py`, or the freezing step capturing the wrong config file) before relying on any `.runs/*/*.yaml` file for provenance.

- R11: Gemma-4 12B (`gemma4_unified`) + vLLM → **needs vLLM ≥ v0.23.0** (verified 2026-07-06 against the model registry: `Gemma4Unified*` absent through v0.22.1, present in v0.23.0/v0.24.0). The pinned serving image (`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0`) and `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) both fail at ModelConfig validation ("model type `gemma4_unified` ... does not recognize this architecture"). On L4 24GB the BF16 checkpoint can never fit regardless (11.96B params ≈ 23.9 GB weights > 22.03 GiB usable) — use `RedHatAI/gemma-4-12B-it-FP8-Dynamic` (~12.75 GB load, compressed-tensors FP8, tested by RedHat against vLLM nightly) with `VLLM_IMAGE=vllm/vllm-openai:v0.24.0`. Verified serving on L4 (2026-07-06): fp8 KV, `--max-num-batched-tokens 4096`, 50,087-token KV cache at 8K max_model_len; tool calling via parser `gemma4` works; also verified with LMCache + `--enforce-eager`. Preset in `.env.example`.

The contamination rate for what checkpoint-1000 actually trained on (see below) is 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test.

input.old_string:

lines: - R8: Gemma4 + TurboQuant → **works with perf caveat**. Two hooks in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py`: (1) `_install_gemma4_mixed_backend_hook` bypasses the TRITON_ATTN global force at `vllm/model_executor/models/config.py:100`; (2) `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` auto-injects `enforce_eager=True` for Gemma-4 + `turboquant_*`, skipping vLLM v1's broken mixed-KV profiler (`gpu_model_runner.py:6598`). CUDA graphs off → expect ~15-25% decode throughput penalty vs graph-mode. Numerical correctness unaffected. Restore graph-mode once upstream lands a per-layer-aware profiler.

- R9: Gemma-4 (26B-A4B, 31B) + Unsloth `fast_inference=True` → **architecture allowlist rejection**. Unsloth 2026.5.2's `VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at `unsloth/models/vision.py:242` is hardcoded to `{qwen2_5_vl, gemma3, mistral3, qwen3_vl, qwen3_vl_moe}`. Gemma-4 ships as a SigLIP+Gemma4 multimodal stack, so `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(..., fast_inference=True)` raises `RuntimeError: Found architectures: siglip, gemma4!` (`unsloth/models/vision.py:610`). **Auto-resolved.** `training/grpo.py::_detect_model_family` reads the SFT checkpoint's `adapter_config.json → base_model_name_or_path` and resolves `AutoConfig.model_type`. When the family is in `UNSLOTH_VLLM_INCOMPATIBLE_FAMILIES = {"gemma4"}`, `use_vllm` is forced to `False` with a `vllm_rollout_disabled_unsloth_incompat` warning; training proceeds with HF `model.generate()` rollouts (~31 s/step on H100 80GB → ~8.6 h per 1000-step GRPO run). Other Cat A models (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Mistral-Small-3.1-24B, Qwen3.6 variants) are unaffected — they continue to get Unsloth colocate vLLM rollouts. To re-enable vLLM rollouts on Gemma-4: (a) wait for Unsloth to add `gemma4` to the allowlist (file an issue at github.com/unslothai/unsloth), (b) switch to external vLLM server mode (`vllm_mode="server"` + a separate `trl vllm-serve` process — tight on 80GB but viable with FP8 weights), or (c) monkey-patch `unsloth.models.vision.VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at import time (quick to try, may fail deeper in vLLM-engine init).

- R10: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B + vLLM → **no tagged release supports it** (verified 2026-06-11: vLLM 0.22.1 model registry has no `diffusion_gemma` entry; transformers added the model defs in 5.11.0; the HF checkpoints ship no remote code). Serving works ONLY via vLLM's `dgemma` branch (PR #45163, open) — prebuilt images `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma-cu129` (CUDA 12.9) / `:gemma` (CUDA 13.0, driver ≥ 580). Required flags per the RedHatAI model card: `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--max-num-seqs 4`, `--hf-overrides '{"diffusion_sampler": "entropy_bound", "diffusion_entropy_bound": 0.1}'`. Wired into `deployments/models/` (`VLLM_IMAGE` + `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER` + `VLLM_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE` knobs; preset in `.env.example`). `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) cannot serve it — fails at ModelConfig validation ("model type `diffusion_gemma` ... not recognize this architecture"). Keep `kv-cache-dtype` blank (fp8 KV on the diffusion decode path unvalidated). Re-pin to a tagged image once PR #45163 merges into a release.

Fix: investigate why the frozen config's `data.source` doesn't match the resolved path actually passed to the trainer (e.g. a default-path fallback in `training/sft.py`, or the freezing step capturing the wrong config file) before relying on any `.runs/*/*.yaml` file for provenance.

lines: - R9: Gemma-4 (26B-A4B, 31B) + Unsloth `fast_inference=True` → **architecture allowlist rejection**. Unsloth 2026.5.2's `VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at `unsloth/models/vision.py:242` is hardcoded to `{qwen2_5_vl, gemma3, mistral3, qwen3_vl, qwen3_vl_moe}`. Gemma-4 ships as a SigLIP+Gemma4 multimodal stack, so `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(..., fast_inference=True)` raises `RuntimeError: Found architectures: siglip, gemma4!` (`unsloth/models/vision.py:610`). **Auto-resolved.** `training/grpo.py::_detect_model_family` reads the SFT checkpoint's `adapter_config.json → base_model_name_or_path` and resolves `AutoConfig.model_type`. When the family is in `UNSLOTH_VLLM_INCOMPATIBLE_FAMILIES = {"gemma4"}`, `use_vllm` is forced to `False` with a `vllm_rollout_disabled_unsloth_incompat` warning; training proceeds with HF `model.generate()` rollouts (~31 s/step on H100 80GB → ~8.6 h per 1000-step GRPO run). Other Cat A models (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Mistral-Small-3.1-24B, Qwen3.6 variants) are unaffected — they continue to get Unsloth colocate vLLM rollouts. To re-enable vLLM rollouts on Gemma-4: (a) wait for Unsloth to add `gemma4` to the allowlist (file an issue at github.com/unslothai/unsloth), (b) switch to external vLLM server mode (`vllm_mode="server"` + a separate `trl vllm-serve` process — tight on 80GB but viable with FP8 weights), or (c) monkey-patch `unsloth.models.vision.VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM` at import time (quick to try, may fail deeper in vLLM-engine init).

- R10: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B + vLLM → **no tagged release supports it** (verified 2026-06-11: vLLM 0.22.1 model registry has no `diffusion_gemma` entry; transformers added the model defs in 5.11.0; the HF checkpoints ship no remote code). Serving works ONLY via vLLM's `dgemma` branch (PR #45163, open) — prebuilt images `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma-cu129` (CUDA 12.9) / `:gemma` (CUDA 13.0, driver ≥ 580). Required flags per the RedHatAI model card: `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--max-num-seqs 4`, `--hf-overrides '{"diffusion_sampler": "entropy_bound", "diffusion_entropy_bound": 0.1}'`. Wired into `deployments/models/` (`VLLM_IMAGE` + `VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER` + `VLLM_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE` knobs; preset in `.env.example`). `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) cannot serve it — fails at ModelConfig validation ("model type `diffusion_gemma` ... not recognize this architecture"). Keep `kv-cache-dtype` blank (fp8 KV on the diffusion decode path unvalidated). Re-pin to a tagged image once PR #45163 merges into a release.

- R11: Gemma-4 12B (`gemma4_unified`) + vLLM → **needs vLLM ≥ v0.23.0** (verified 2026-07-06 against the model registry: `Gemma4Unified*` absent through v0.22.1, present in v0.23.0/v0.24.0). The pinned serving image (`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0`) and `serving/launch_vllm.sh`'s `.venv-infer` (vLLM 0.20.0) both fail at ModelConfig validation ("model type `gemma4_unified` ... does not recognize this architecture"). On L4 24GB the BF16 checkpoint can never fit regardless (11.96B params ≈ 23.9 GB weights > 22.03 GiB usable) — use `RedHatAI/gemma-4-12B-it-FP8-Dynamic` (~12.75 GB load, compressed-tensors FP8, tested by RedHat against vLLM nightly) with `VLLM_IMAGE=vllm/vllm-openai:v0.24.0`. Verified serving on L4 (2026-07-06): fp8 KV, `--max-num-batched-tokens 4096`, 50,087-token KV cache at 8K max_model_len; tool calling via parser `gemma4` works; also verified with LMCache + `--enforce-eager`. Preset in `.env.example`.

+- R12: Cat A SFT corpus role corruption — messages with malformed roles like `assistant to=tool` / `assistant to=book_reservation` and empty `content` (likely leaked teacher-model tool-routing syntax that failed to parse into `<tool_call>` blocks), present at every stage of the Task A pipeline: `data/output/sft/task_a_splits` (canonical, DVC-tracked: 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test conversations affected) and worse in `data/output/grpo/task_a` (L3-L5 filtered, what the held-out audit draws from: 17.2% train / **22.4% validation**). 62.9% occur immediately after a `tool`-role message. Root cause pinned to `scripts/clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record`'s `_is_role_confused()`, which only strips `tool`-role messages containing literal `<tool_call>` text — a different bug — and has no logic for a corrupted `role` field itself; `scripts/split_task_a_sft.py` and `scripts/filter_grpo_data.py` don't validate roles either, so it passes through every stage untouched. `data/data_validator.py` only validates that `messages[0]` is role `system`. Gemma-4's chat template does not reject unknown roles — it renders them literally (`chat_template.jinja:219`), so under `loss_mask: all_tokens` the model trains with full gradient weight on these degenerate empty turns. Discovered 2026-07-14 during forensics on the "announce-but-don't-call" tool-emission gap (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1). Fix: patch `clean_task_a_sft.py::clean_record` to drop/strip any message whose role isn't in `{system, user, assistant, tool}`, then re-run the DVC pipeline (`task_a_sft` → `task_a_sft_splits` → `task_a_grpo`) — not a one-off patch to a static file. The contamination rate for what checkpoint-1000 actually trained on is 5.1% train / 5.7% validation / 4.6% test (confirmed via R13's `train.log` cross-check).

+- R13: `.runs/sft_cat_a/sft_cat_a.yaml` provenance bug — the frozen config recorded for checkpoint-1000 claims `data.source: data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned/splits` (an untracked, non-canonical, half-sized snapshot; not a declared DVC output), but `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/train.log` proves the actual run loaded 9,131 train / 1,074 eval examples — an exact match for the canonical, DVC-tracked `data/output/sft/task_a_splits`, not the 4,414/519 the frozen config implies. So checkpoint-1000 **was** trained on the correct canonical dataset; the frozen run-config snapshot mechanism itself is unreliable and should not be trusted as ground truth for "what data trained this checkpoint" without cross-checking `train.log`. Discovered 2026-07-14 (see `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` §5.1 second correction). Fix: investigate why the frozen config's `data.source` doesn't match the resolved path actually passed to the trainer (e.g. a default-path fallback in `training/sft.py`, or the freezing step capturing the wrong config file) before relying on any `.runs/*/*.yaml` file for provenance.
